title: Chargeback Reconciliation Closure
description: A reconciliation and back-office workflow that resolves breaks, validates accounting alignment, and closes audit-critical exceptions. Focus case: chargeback reconciliation closure.
assumptions: Each chargeback_case is uniquely identified and deterministic for the workflow lifecycle.
Source transactions are treated as immutable reference data for matching operations; reconciliations rely on a stable snapshot.
Required evidence_documents and approver attestations are available within configured SLAs before closure actions.
Automated matching rules produce candidate associations; human analyst review is available for exceptions exceeding confidence thresholds.
Accounting policies (accounting_policy) are pre-configured and deterministically applied by the workflow.
risks: Missing or delayed evidence_document leads to prolonged case age and audit exposure.
Incorrect association of source_transaction or external_reference resulting in improper accounting adjustments and misstated balances.
Over-reliance on manual analyst judgement increases operational variability and audit query volume.
Reversal operations are applied without full traceability, compromising the audit trail for prior adjustments.
Integration failures with external systems (clearing partners) prevent final R18/R39 cross-references and block closure.
